ab: kosi’a ki tatavi nakowa si kake ka amowa kiname swapepeale tatavane.legeageane.
ch: 昨天我和爸爸去拜訪我的叔叔。
en: Yesterday, my father and I visited my uncle.

ab: kidra ka matikaykay namiya, da’ane cai ka maboto.
ch: 他告訴我們，房子的屋頂破了一個洞。
en: He told us that there was a hole in the roof of his house.

ab: ’ako syozingini cai ka, syalikai cai ’ievese molili.
ch: 他在修理屋頂時，從屋頂摔下來了。
en: He fell off the roof while repairing it.

ab: ki tatava ka wamece na nwange na bwate si thaviki bwa’ay tatavane.legeageane.
ch: 爸爸帶了牛肉和檳榔給叔叔。
en: My father brought beef and betel nuts for my uncle.

ab: kake si tatava ki legeageay ka ’iyakay kiname tasyaekane mababaka, kini ka lapatikaykaykay na mawlay.
ch: 我和叔叔在房間裡聊天，他分享了很多故事。
en: My uncle and I chatted in the room, and he shared many stories.

ab: ko ’abee myali ka, ki tatava. legeageay ka pakileve nakowa patekedrenga koso mya, lakagi’i na engedelaniso mya.
ch: 我要離開前，叔叔誇我長高了，也變得更帥氣了。
en: Before I left, my uncle praised me for growing taller and becoming more handsome.

ab: la’apobaka kake, no magi’ininga drapalini ki tatava legeageay ka, nobilanenga ka ni’awkay da’aniname ’aangi.
ch: 我說：「等叔叔腳好了，下次也來我們家玩。」
en: I said, “When your foot gets better, come to our house next time and visit us.”
